requirementsAuditDisposition.note: The accepted 17-family authority/writer/schema/atomicity/corruption/backup catalog is semantically unchanged.
All 12 operations retain honest ADR-29 coordination classes and unresolved writers remain fail-closed.
The marker-owned external-process negative control again reports lostExternalUpdate=true.
Production TeamBackupService is now constructed and fault-characterized by TB-01 through TB-12.
Supported Online Backup again proves active WAL, independent 2,000-row reopen/integrity, and fail-closed BUSY/corruption/deadline handling.
Final-image ABI, production async worker wire, and deployment quiescence require W6/controller integration and are deliberately outside this W3-only remediation.
The handoff explicitly maps W3/W5 as overlapping decompositions under EST-RECOVERY-STATE; controller adoption remains outside this lane.

findingResolutions.resolution: A marker-owned 12-case fixture now constructs the production TeamBackupService and asserts async/sync enumeration, config readiness, copy/error swallowing, stale/retention prune, identity mutation, split manifest/registry publication failures, corrupt-registry rebuild, and missing/corrupt/partial/mtime restore behavior.
findingResolutions.result: 7 tests passed; 12 characterized cases; legacy_unverified classification retained

findingResolutions.resolution: The remediation handoff records an explicit no-index whitespace check over all 16 entirely untracked W3 additions instead of relying on plain git diff --check.
findingResolutions.result: all handoff-listed additions checked; zero whitespace findings

provider ownership/flush guarantees for members/** and transcript artifacts
legacy config/launch paths that bypass the intended coordinator

backupFaultResults.detail: 7 tests assert 12 marker-owned cases; observed split publications and best-effort paths retain legacy_unverified classification
better-sqlite3#backup completed with live -wal, independent reopen returned 2,000 rows and integrity_check=ok
child replacement completed but stale app rename erased its update; lostExternalUpdate=true
fault-injected backup adapter returned typed backup_busy and partial DB/WAL/SHM outputs were absent
invalid SQLite source returned source_corrupt before destination publication
progress deadline returned backup_deadline and removed partial output

unverifiedClaims: The SQLite native addon was exercised on the current Linux Node ABI, not the final packaged Electron ABI/container artifact.
No real provider was launched; provider/version writer identities require W2 sanitized fixtures.
A deterministic real-lock Online Backup SQLITE_BUSY was not claimed; BUSY was injected at the Database#backup adapter boundary.
The standalone spike does not prove the production worker async wire operation, which is outside this Phase 0 W3 remediation.
No deployment recovery point was built; final-image ABI, writer drain, watcher watermarks, immutable publication, credential exclusion, and restore remain W6/controller gates.

adrRecommendations.reason: future SQLite user_version currently does not close known-table mutation
unverified file writes remain team-scoped external actors; run attribution needs provider evidence
negative fixture proves app-only locking cannot support a lossless external-writer claim
Online Backup API is feasible and production TeamBackupService is fault-characterized as legacy_unverified; only quiesced verified publication can be a deployment recovery point

estimateReconciliation.controllerAction: deduplicate once under the shared EST-RECOVERY-STATE range
